relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Cornelius appears in Caesarea as a centurion.
His household reverence, prayer, and mercy rise before the Divine.
At prayer hour, a messenger speaks his name.
Prayer and mercy become memorial before the Divine.
He is told to summon Peter from Joppa.
Peter lodges with Simon the tanner by the sea.
Cornelius sends trusted servants and a devout soldier.
The vision becomes obedient action.
Peter ascends to the roof for prayer.
Hunger and trance open the vision.
Heaven opens and a great sheet descends.
Creatures of every kind fill the vessel.
The voice commands rise, slaughter, eat.
Peter resists from purity boundaries.
What the Divine cleansed must not be made common.
Threefold vision seals the lesson.
Peter is perplexed as Cornelius’ men arrive.
They ask for Simon Peter.
The Spirit interprets the timing.
Peter is told to go without distinction.
Peter receives the seekers.
Cornelius is witnessed as righteous and reverent.
Peter lodges them and takes siblings with him.
Cornelius gathers relatives and friends in expectation.
Cornelius falls at Peter’s feet.
Peter raises him: I too am human.
Peter enters and finds many gathered.
The vision’s meaning: call no human common or unclean.
Peter came without objection and asks why he was summoned.
Cornelius recounts the bright messenger.
Prayer heard, mercy remembered.
Peter by the sea was named for summons.
The household gathers before the Divine to hear the commanded word.
Peter grasps that the Divine shows no partiality.
Every nation can offer reverence and justice acceptable to the Divine.
Peace comes through Jesus Messiah, Lord of all.
The story is known from Galilee onward.
Jesus is anointed with Spirit and power to heal oppression.
The apostles witness deeds, death, and tree.
The Divine raised him on the third day.
Chosen witnesses ate and drank with the risen one.
They testify to the appointed judge of living and dead.
The prophets witness release through his name for all who trust.
While the word is still spoken, holy Spirit falls on the hearers.
Circumcised believers are amazed that Spirit is poured on the nations.
Tongues and magnifying the Divine confirm the gift.
Water cannot be withheld from Spirit-receivers.
They are baptized in Jesus Messiah’s name and ask Peter to remain.
